danger_signals.visual_form: Blue sea urchin sprite with spiky appearance
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to player; contact causes damage and loss of life

danger_signals.visual_form: Dark vortex/void spatial anomalies in maze
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; falling into black holes results in instant life loss
Temporal pressure; running out of time ends the stage and causes life loss

danger_signals.visual_form: Unira sprite undergoes visual state change after Sound Wave attack
danger_signals.meaning: Brief vulnerability window; stunned enemies can be pushed into walls for points

safety_signals.visual_form: Pole/post markers distributed throughout maze layout
safety_signals.meaning: Navigation anchor points; only mechanism for Bubbles to change direction safely

safety_signals.visual_form: Shiny golden treasure shapes (heart or mushroom silhouettes)
safety_signals.meaning: Objective completion; revealed ingots represent progress and safe puzzle solution

safety_signals.visual_form: Wave effect emanating from Bubbles character
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary protection mechanic; stuns enemies and enables offensive repositioning

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Appears in all maze stages; player-controlled protagonist seeking treasure recovery

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Sea urchin spike pattern; distinctive spiky silhouette
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Boss Unira variant exists in arcade expanded edition (VS. Clu Clu Land)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Roaming patrol entities within maze; narrative role as treasure thief

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Golden ingots shaped as hearts and mushrooms
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underwater kingdom aesthetic; maze-enclosed arenas; treasure distribution patterns

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Ingot uncovered state (odd pass threshold not met)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ingots display dull/obscured appearance; appear as puzzle outline
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must traverse over ingots multiple times (odd number) to toggle reveal state; mechanical puzzle tension

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Golden ingot transforms to bright, shiny appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Stage progress indicator; contributes to level completion condition

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Unira disappears from maze after wall collision
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Threat eliminated from current arena; point reward granted to player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Flashing effect occurs (later Switch Online version slows this effect)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Transition trigger; advances to next puzzle level
Game loop reset; player returns to stage selection or restart

environmental_storytelling.example: Golden ingots form recognizable shapes (hearts, mushrooms) that reveal progressive picture patterns, embedding puzzle solution within environmental visual design
Turning Posts force specific navigation paths, encoding the puzzle logic into physical maze layout rather than explicit instructions
Blue Unira visually distinct from red Bubbles and golden treasures; establishes visual hierarchy of threat vs. objective
Balloonfish protagonist and sea urchin antagonist establish cohesive aquatic setting; environmental theme reinforces narrative context of undersea treasure theft

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Continuous rotation around turning posts; momentum-based navigation requiring player input at specific pole locations
Life counter decrements; visual feedback (sprite disruption) indicates collision with Unira or Black Hole
Active movement through maze; poses constant threat; forces player evasion routing
Temporary immobilization; becomes pushable into walls by Bubbles; visual state change indicates vulnerability window
Removed from play space; point reward triggers; reduces active threats in arena

notes: Clu Clu Land employs minimal but effective visual communication suited to puzzle paradigm. Primary danger signal is enemy color contrast (blue vs. red); safety derives from structural navigation anchors (posts). World state transitions are marked by visual effects (flashing on clear, ingot appearance toggle). The game lacks traditional faction politics but establishes clear antagonism through character roles and narrative framing (Unira as thief, Bubbles as hero). Environmental storytelling is subtle—embedded in maze structure and treasure shape progression rather than explicit environmental markers. Boss Unira variant in arcade edition provides difficulty scaling visual indicator.
